title: BACKND Base notify and realtime handler flows
summary: Covers realtime notification server connectivity, authorization callbacks, connection-state checks, and handler categories for user, console, and other notification events.
capabilities: route realtime notification questions to the correct connection or handler page
explain the difference between connecting to the notify server and receiving specific handler events
highlight authorization and reconnection concerns in notification workflows
identify whether a request is about user handlers, console handlers, or connection lifecycle state
prerequisites: SDK initialization and a valid access token context are available before connecting when authentication is required
The relevant notification features are enabled in the BACKND console if actual events are expected
The client sets handlers before or around the connection lifecycle as required by the current docs
common_pitfalls: Assuming a successful socket connection means the desired notification feature is enabled
Ignoring authorization failure reasons such as bad clientAppId or expired accessToken
Mixing connection lifecycle logic with domain-specific handlers without checking the current official page
verification_strategy: Use https://docs.backnd.com plus the narrower source route before generating production event wiring or callback code.
Treat this topic as a route selector and risk summary, not as an immutable callback reference.
public_notes: Realtime handler names and connection behavior should be revalidated from the official docs before generating production event wiring.
This topic helps agents choose the correct notify sub-area before they fetch exact callback details.
